description: Five SEC 10-K extraction & calculation questions. Same 5 docs serve as evidence pool — each question has a different correct doc and four distractors. Mix of straight retrieval and multi-step finance calculation; agents commonly fumble the calculations.

questions.question: What is Netflix's year end FY2017 total current liabilities (in USD millions)? Source documents are in ./docs/ — exactly one is the right Netflix filing, the rest are distractors. Read the document, identify the line item, and report the final number on the last line of your response.

What is AES's FY2022 return on assets (ROA)? ROA is defined as: FY2022 net income / (average total assets between FY2021 and FY2022). Round your answer to two decimal places. Source documents are in ./docs/ — exactly one is the right AES filing, the rest are distractors. Report the final number on the last line of your response.

What is the year end FY2018 net property, plant & equipment (net PP&E) for 3M, in USD billions? Source documents are in ./docs/ — exactly one is the right 3M filing, the rest are distractors. Report the final number on the last line of your response.

What is FY2018 days payable outstanding (DPO) for Walmart? DPO is defined as: 365 * (average accounts payable between FY2017 and FY2018) / (FY2018 COGS + change in inventory between FY2017 and FY2018). Round to two decimal places. Source documents are in ./docs/ — exactly one is the right Walmart filing, the rest are distractors. Report the final number on the last line of your response.

What is Block's (formerly known as Square) FY2016 working capital ratio? Define working capital ratio as total current assets divided by total current liabilities. Round to two decimal places. Source documents are in ./docs/ — exactly one is the right Block filing, the rest are distractors. Report the final number on the last line of your response.
